2604.00842.title: Proactive Agent Research Environment: Simulating Active Users to Evaluate Proactive Assistants
2604.00842.paper_summary: Proactive agents that anticipate user needs and autonomously execute tasks hold great promise as digital assistants, yet the lack of realistic user simulation frameworks hinders their development. Existing approaches model apps as flat tool-calling APIs, failing to capture the stateful and sequential nature of user interaction in digital environments and making realistic user simulation infeasible. We introduce Proactive Agent Research Environment (Pare), a framework for building and evaluating proactive agents in digital environments. Pare models applications as finite state machines with stateful navigation and state-dependent action space for the user simulator, enabling active user simulation. Building on this foundation, we present Pare-Bench, a benchmark of 143 diverse tasks spanning communication, productivity, scheduling, and lifestyle apps, designed to test context observation, goal inference, intervention timing, and multi-app orchestration.
2604.00842.paper_summary_zh: 主動代理人能夠預測用戶需求並自主執行任務，作為數位助手具有很大潛力，但現實的用戶模擬框架的缺乏阻礙了它們的發展。現有的方法將應用程式建模為平面的工具調用API，未能捕捉數位環境中用戶互動的狀態性和序列性，從而使得現實的用戶模擬變得不可行。我們介紹了主動代理研究環境（Pare），這是一個用於在數位環境中構建和評估主動代理的框架。Pare將應用程式建模為具有狀態導航和狀態依賴行動空間的有限狀態機，從而實現主動用戶模擬。在此基礎上，我們提出了Pare-Bench，這是一個涵蓋通信、生產力、排程和生活方式應用的143個多樣化任務的基準，旨在測試上下文觀察、目標推斷、干預時機和多應用協同。
2604.00842.author: Deepak Nathani et.al.
2604.00842.authors: Deepak Nathani, Cheng Zhang, Chang Huan, Jiaming Shan, Yinfei Yang, Alkesh Patel, Zhe Gan, William Yang Wang, Michael Saxon, Xin Eric Wang
